MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: Revenue compounding at 65-70 % YoY through Q1 FY27; China now permanently zero; customer concentration (4-way >10 %) unchanged; supply still gated by energy/power, not chips.

shifts.note: Revenue growth rate re-accelerated from +68 % (FY26) to +69 % YoY in Q1 FY27 as Blackwell full-system ramp continues.
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66 % (Q3 FY26) → +68 % (FY26 full year) → +73 % (Q1 FY27); LTM revenue ~$215.9 B

shifts.note: Gross margin compressed 2-3 pts YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within mgmt 71-73 % target band.
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 73.4 % (Q3 FY26) → 73.5 % (FY26 full year) → 71.1 % (Q1 FY27)

shifts.note: Four customers each >10 % of revenue across all three filings — no dilution of concentration despite revenue scale.
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers >10 % (22/15/13/11 %); Q1 FY27 repeated same disclosure — structural, not transient

shifts.note: China revenue is now permanently zero; H20 license revoked; $4.5 B inventory charge taken in Q1 FY27.
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0 B → FY26 full-year H20 effectively zero after $4.5 B Q1 FY27 charge; no recovery pathway modelled

shifts.note: Constraint language unchanged across all three filings — energy and data-center capacity, not chip supply, is gating.
shifts.trend: All three filings cite data-center energy and capital availability as the deployment gating constraint — no easing language

shifts.note: Vendor-financing and infrastructure investment footprint growing; watch FCF translation.
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K: $17.5 B private/infra investments + $3.5 B land/power guarantees; Q1 FY27 adds further DC lease obligations

NVDA.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — revenue growth re-accelerated to +73 % YoY in Q1 FY27; China optionality fully impaired and now a zero-revenue line; customer concentration (4-way >10 %) is structural. Supply gating by energy/power availability means hyperscaler capex budgets, not NVDA production, determine the ceiling.

CSCO.covers: FQ2 FY26 8-K (Feb 2026) → FQ3 FY26 8-K (May 2026) → FQ3 FY26 10-Q (May 2026)
CSCO.headline: AI infrastructure orders accelerating within a still-modest 5-9 % revenue growth envelope; Splunk integration on track; networking order cycle recovering.

shifts.note: Revenue reaccelerated from +5 % (FQ2) to +9 % (FQ3) — AI networking orders are the catalyst.
shifts.trend: FQ2 FY26 revenue $14.0 B (+5 % YoY) → FQ3 FY26 revenue $14.1 B (+9 % YoY) — first reacceleration in 2 years

shifts.note: AI infrastructure (ethernet switching for AI fabrics) is the fastest-growing product line; Splunk ARR building.
shifts.trend: AI ethernet orders growing double-digits; Splunk ARR on track for $3.5 B+ FY27 target

shifts.note: Gross margin stable at 64.7-64.9 %; software mix (Splunk) providing floor.
shifts.trend: GM 64.7 % (FQ2) → 64.9 % (FQ3) — stable; Splunk mix accreting long-term

shifts.note: FY26 revenue and EPS guidance raised twice; AI networking momentum driving upward revisions.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised at both FQ2 and FQ3 on AI infrastructure order momentum

CSCO.verdict: Cisco is not an AI-native compounder, but AI networking demand is reversing the post-pandemic order digestion cycle. Splunk integration is the margin-structure bet; AI ethernet (high-radix switching) is the growth bet. Revenue growth modest but re-accelerating.

ANET.headline: AI-ethernet switching is sustaining +20-25 % YoY revenue growth; hyperscaler back-end network refresh is the dominant demand driver; margins near record.

shifts.note: Revenue growth re-accelerated from +20 % (FY25) to +25 % (Q4 2025) as AI back-end ethernet demand surged.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 revenue $1.93 B (+25 % YoY); FY25 revenue $7.2 B (+20 % YoY); Q1 2026 sustained ~25 %

shifts.note: Gross margin holding near record ~64-65 %; software (EOS, CloudVision) mix improving floor.
shifts.trend: GM 64-65 % across both filings — pricing power maintained on AI switching refresh cycle

shifts.note: Management raised FY2026 guidance at Q4 2025 and maintained/raised at Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: FY2026 revenue growth guided high-teens → raised given Q1 2026 beat

shifts.note: Top hyperscalers (Microsoft, Meta, Google, Amazon) dominate order mix — revenue concentration increasing.
shifts.trend: Hyperscaler AI back-end is now majority of incremental growth; enterprise/campus secondary

ANET.verdict: AI-ethernet thesis executing — Arista is capturing the back-end network spend of the largest AI training clusters in the world. Margins near record, guidance rising, hyperscaler customer concentration is the only risk.

VRT.headline: Beat-and-raise cadence intact — liquid-cooling orders growing >100 % YoY; FY2026 guidance raised twice; pricing power compounding.

shifts.note: Revenue growth re-accelerating as liquid-cooling (CDU) mixes up vs. air-cooling.
shifts.trend: FY2025 revenue +29 % YoY; Q1 2026 sustained elevated growth; FY2026 guidance $9.8-10.0 B

shifts.note: Gross margin expansion accelerating — liquid-cooling product mix and pricing power compounding.
shifts.trend: Gross margin expanding QoQ as CDU (higher margin) mixes up; FY2026 guidance includes further expansion

shifts.note: FY2026 guidance raised at Q4 2025 and again at Q1 2026 — consecutive raise cadence.
shifts.trend: FY2026 organic revenue growth guided 20 %+; adjusted EPS guidance raised twice

shifts.note: CDU and PDU lead times >52 weeks — supply is tight, not demand; backlog at records.
shifts.trend: Backlog records both quarters; lead times extending on liquid-cooling products

VRT.verdict: Liquid-cooling thesis executing faster than expected — Vertiv is the clearest beneficiary of AI GPU rack density forcing thermal architecture change. Beat-and-raise cadence, expanding margins, and multi-year backlog visibility make this the highest-conviction power/cooling holding.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed — CEG is now the largest US competitive generator; AI data-center PPA pipeline multi-gigawatt; nuclear production tax credits ($500 M/yr) de-risking the thesis.

shifts.note: Calpine (+27 GW gas fleet) transforms the revenue base; AI PPA pipeline adds organic growth.
shifts.trend: Revenue flat/down organically on generation mix; Calpine close adds $6-8 B+ pro-forma revenue run-rate

shifts.note: Calpine diversifies CEG revenue away from pure nuclear — adds gas generation and retail.
shifts.trend: Nuclear-only → nuclear + 27 GW gas + retail electricity; customer diversification improving

shifts.note: Calpine integration capex plus ongoing nuclear relicensing and new AI PPA infrastructure investment.
shifts.trend: Post-Calpine leverage ~4.5× net debt/EBITDA; deleveraging target 3.0× by 2028

shifts.note: Management raised FY2026 adjusted operating earnings guidance on Calpine contribution and IRA nuclear PTCs.
shifts.trend: FY2026 adj. operating earnings guidance raised; nuclear PTC ~$500 M/yr newly visible in guidance

CEG.verdict: CEG is the cleanest AI power thesis in the market — nuclear baseload plus Calpine gas dispatchability is exactly what hyperscalers need. PTC ($500 M/yr) provides earnings floor; AI PPA pipeline is the upside. Leverage (4.5×) is the key risk to monitor.

AMD.headline: Instinct GPU revenue exceeded $5 B in FY2025 and is tracking higher in 2026; EPYC server CPU momentum sustained; embedded recovery still lagging.

shifts.note: Data-center GPU revenue trajectory rising: Q4 2025 $3.7 B → Q1 2026 tracking higher.
shifts.trend: Full-year 2025 DC GPU >$5 B; Q1 2026 DC GPU growing further; EPYC server CPU holding share

shifts.note: Data-center accelerating; client stable; embedded recovery extending; gaming softer.
shifts.trend: DC GPU dominant growth driver; embedded recovery guided H2 2026; gaming deprioritised

shifts.note: Instinct GPU guidance raised at both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 earnings.
shifts.trend: DC GPU guidance raised sequentially; CDNA4 (MI400) on TSMC N3 tape-out completed

shifts.note: HBM supply from SK Hynix and Micron is the primary constraint on MI300X volume.
shifts.trend: HBM3 allocation secured for 2026; TSMC N5 capacity for MI300X also secured

AMD.verdict: AMD GPU thesis is intact — $5 B+ annual run-rate achieved, guidance rising, and CDNA4 roadmap on track. Execution risk is HBM supply (constrained) and NVDA competitive response on pricing. Embedded recovery is the lagging leg.

AVGO.headline: AI semiconductor revenue trajectory: $12.2 B in FY25 → $4.1 B in Q1 FY26 alone; custom XPU TAM raised to $60-90 B by FY27; VMware software margin accretion on track.

shifts.note: AI revenue quarterly run-rate accelerating from FY25 $3 B/Q average to $4.1 B in Q1 FY26.
shifts.trend: FY25 AI semis $12.2 B ($3 B/Q avg) → Q1 FY26 AI semis $4.1 B — +37 % QoQ acceleration

shifts.note: VMware subscription conversion accreting blended gross margin above 79 %.
shifts.trend: Gross margin >79 % both quarters; VMware perpetual-to-SaaS conversion adding ~3-4 pts

shifts.note: AI TAM raised from $60-90 B (FY27) at FQ4; Q1 FY26 guided $14.9 B — above prior consensus.
shifts.trend: FQ4: TAM $60-90 B at top-3 hyperscalers; Q1 FY26 beat and raised Q2 guidance

shifts.note: Google and Meta are the primary XPU customers — two-customer concentration risk.
shifts.trend: Google TPU v6 and Meta MTIA2 are dominant XPU programs; Amazon secondary

AVGO.verdict: AVGO is executing the highest-conviction custom-ASIC build-out in semiconductors. $4.1 B/quarter AI revenue run-rate is already at the top of most analyst 2026 models. VMware software margin accretion is a free option on top of the AI ASIC thesis. Customer concentration (Google + Meta) is the primary risk.

MRVL.headline: Custom AI XPU revenue approaching $1 B/quarter; Data Infrastructure tripling YoY; guidance substantially above consensus — fastest-improving thesis in the dashboard.

shifts.note: Data Infrastructure revenue went from growing rapidly (Q3) to tripling YoY (Q4) — XPU ramp is real.
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26: DC/AI revenue growing; Q4 FY26: Data Infrastructure 'tripling YoY' — XPU approaching $1 B/Q

shifts.note: Q4 FY26 guidance substantially raised vs. prior consensus; FY27 AI revenue outlook dramatically higher.
shifts.trend: Q3 → Q4: guidance raised materially; FY27 AI data-infrastructure outlook now $8-10 B+ per analysts

shifts.note: Amazon Trainium and Google TPU are the dominant XPU programs — two-customer dependency.
shifts.trend: Amazon + Google XPU are majority of AI revenue; diversification into a 3rd hyperscaler expected FY27

shifts.note: Gross margin expanding as higher-margin custom ASIC mixes up vs. commodity networking silicon.
shifts.trend: GM expanding QoQ as AI custom ASIC revenue mix rises — approaching 60 %+

MRVL.verdict: MRVL is the single best-improving thesis in the dashboard — from a networking semi in recovery to an AI custom silicon compounder in two quarters. XPU programs with Amazon and Google are multi-year, locked in. The risk is that custom ASIC revenue displaces open-market GPU purchases (a risk to NVDA), not to MRVL.

ORCL.headline: OCI growing 52 % YoY; RPO surpassed $130 B in Q3 ($97 B in Q2); AI GPU cluster contracts with hyperscalers converting to revenue — ORCL is becoming an AI cloud infrastructure player.

shifts.note: OCI revenue growth sustained at 52 % YoY for two consecutive quarters — sustained, not transient.
shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 OCI +52 % YoY; Q3 FY26 OCI +52 % YoY; RPO $97 B → $130 B+ in one quarter

shifts.note: RPO growing $10 B+ per quarter — forward revenue visibility exceptional.
shifts.trend: RPO $97 B (Q2) → $130 B+ (Q3) — $33 B added in one quarter, driven by AI cluster contracts

shifts.note: AI GPU cluster customers (xAI, OpenAI, major hyperscalers) concentrating OCI contract mix.
shifts.trend: AI cluster contracts dominating new RPO additions; traditional enterprise DB cloud secondary

shifts.note: OCI scale improving gross margin on fixed-cost absorption; Fusion/NetSuite SaaS accretive.
shifts.trend: GM improving as OCI scales; Fusion/NetSuite SaaS provides floor

ORCL.verdict: Oracle's transformation from legacy database vendor to AI cloud infrastructure is accelerating faster than the market expected. RPO growing $10 B+/quarter is the clearest evidence — enterprise customers are signing long-term AI commitments with OCI. The risk is execution (data-center buildout pace) given the extraordinary growth in contracted backlog.

GOOGL.headline: GCP re-accelerating — from +30 % (Q4 2025) to +28 % (Q1 2026, against a tougher comp); capex raised to $75 B for 2026; Search monetisation of AI Overview intact.

shifts.note: GCP growing ~28-30 % YoY both quarters; slight deceleration on tougher comps but thesis intact.
shifts.trend: GCP Q4 2025 +30 % YoY → GCP Q1 2026 +28 % YoY; AI services contributing majority of incremental growth

shifts.note: Capex raised from ~$56 B (FY25) to $75 B (FY26 guidance) — largest single-year raise.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 capex $14 B → Q1 2026 capex ~$17-18 B run-rate; FY26 guided $75 B

shifts.note: Operating margin stable ~30-32 % despite heavy capex — cloud economics absorbing investment.
shifts.trend: Operating margin stable across both quarters; GCP operating profit now positive

shifts.note: No China revenue dependency (unlike NVDA) — geopolitical risk is indirect (ad spending slowdown).
shifts.trend: Google revenue not exposed to China export controls; trade-war impact limited to macro ad-spend

GOOGL.verdict: Google is the most defensive hyperscaler in a China-export-control environment — no direct GPU export exposure. GCP AI services are driving re-acceleration. The $75 B capex commitment is the largest AI infrastructure bet outside of NVDA supply contracts.

MSFT.headline: Azure AI contribution growing from 12 to 13 ppts of Azure growth; Copilot seat adoption accelerating; capex run-rating ~$80 B annually — AI investment commitment intensifying.

shifts.note: Azure AI contribution growing from 12 ppts (Q2) to 13 ppts (Q3) of Azure YoY growth.
shifts.trend: Azure +31 % (Q2 FY26) → +33 % (Q3 FY26); AI contribution 12 → 13 ppts

shifts.note: Capex run-rating ~$80 B annually — single largest external AI GPU buyer.
shifts.trend: Q2 capex $20 B → Q3 capex $21 B; FY26 run-rate ~$80 B — above prior guidance

shifts.note: Operating margin stable ~45 % — AI investment absorbed without P&L erosion.
shifts.trend: OM 45 % both quarters; cloud economics protecting margin despite $80 B capex run-rate

shifts.note: Q4 FY26 Azure guidance raised to 34-35 % growth — re-acceleration on capacity additions.
shifts.trend: Azure guided 31 % (Q2) → 33 % (Q3) → 34-35 % (Q4 guide) — accelerating

MSFT.verdict: Microsoft is the largest and most visible AI capex spender — $80 B annual run-rate, growing. Azure AI re-acceleration (AI ppt contribution growing QoQ) validates that the investment is converting to revenue. Copilot seat growth is the enterprise AI adoption canary. No thesis-break risk visible.

META.headline: Ad revenue growing +16-21 % YoY on AI-optimised targeting; capex raised to $64-72 B for FY2026 — two consecutive raises; Llama open-source strategy intact.

shifts.note: Revenue growing 16-21 % YoY on AI ad optimisation — consistent two-quarter trend.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 revenue +21 % YoY → Q1 2026 revenue +16 % YoY (tougher comp, slightly lower but within range)

shifts.note: Capex raised from $60-65 B (Q4 guide) to $64-72 B (Q1 2026 guide) — second consecutive raise.
shifts.trend: FY2025 capex ~$38 B → FY2026 guided $60-65 B → raised $64-72 B — AI infrastructure commitment compounding

shifts.note: Operating margin holding 41-48 % despite capex surge — AI ROI visible in ad revenue.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 OM 48 % → Q1 2026 OM 41 % (seasonal) — structural margin intact

shifts.note: Reality Labs loss stable ~$5 B/yr — maintained as long-term bet; not a near-term thesis driver.
shifts.trend: Reality Labs -$5 B/yr operating loss maintained; Family of Apps generating $45 B+/yr operating income

META.verdict: Meta is the most aggressive AI-capex raisers among hyperscalers — two consecutive raises to $64-72 B for 2026. The thesis is that AI ad targeting ROI more than funds the investment. Llama open-source reduces NVDA dependency. No thesis-break risk; Reality Labs is the perennial overhang.

AMZN.headline: AWS revenue crossed $100 B annually in 2025 and re-accelerating to +17-19 % YoY; Trainium2 and Bedrock gaining enterprise traction; capex $104 B in FY2025.

shifts.note: AWS growth re-accelerating from +17 % (Q4 2025) toward +19 % (Q1 2026 guide).
shifts.trend: AWS Q4 2025 +19 % YoY ($28.8 B) → Q1 2026 guided +17-18 % (tougher comp) — sustained high growth

shifts.note: FY2025 capex $104 B — first time any company crossed $100 B capex in a single year.
shifts.trend: FY2024 capex $75 B → FY2025 capex $104 B (+39 %); Q1 2026 capex $24 B — run-rating ~$96 B

shifts.note: Trainium2 and Bedrock both gaining enterprise traction — reducing NVDA GPU dependency.
shifts.trend: Trainium2 in production; Bedrock multi-model API growing; Graviton4 CPU broadly adopted

shifts.note: AWS operating margin expanding; retail segment margin also improving on cost discipline.
shifts.trend: AWS OM ~38 % and expanding; North America retail OM ~9 %; overall OM expanding

AMZN.verdict: AWS crossed $100 B annual revenue and is re-accelerating — AI workload demand is outpacing even Amazon's extraordinary capex. Trainium2 and Bedrock reduce NVDA GPU concentration risk while expanding the AI services addressable market.

AMKR.headline: AI accelerator packaging growing faster than total company; Arizona advanced packaging facility on track for 2027 revenue; consumer electronics softer.

shifts.note: AI packaging (flip-chip BGA, SiP for AI chips) growing faster than total company in both quarters.
shifts.trend: AI-related packaging growing double-digits; total company revenue recovering from consumer softness

shifts.note: Arizona OSAT facility (TSMC collaboration) groundbreaking completed; Vietnam and Korea capacity expanding.
shifts.trend: Arizona construction on track; capex elevated at $1.5-2.0 B/yr; first Arizona revenue 2027

shifts.note: Consumer electronics (smartphone SiP) soft both quarters; AI packaging offsetting.
shifts.trend: Consumer SiP soft; automotive stable; AI packaging accelerating — mix improving toward AI

shifts.note: Gross margin recovering as AI packaging utilisation rises and consumer mix normalises.
shifts.trend: GM recovering from 2024 lows as AI volumes scale and consumer digestion completes

AMKR.verdict: AMKR is the purest-play advanced packaging story among OSATs — Arizona fab + TSMC collaboration is the key differentiator. Consumer softness is a near-term headwind; AI accelerator packaging growth is the multi-year thesis. Revenue inflection expected when Arizona opens in 2027.

KLAC.headline: Process-control WFE demand sustained — consecutive quarterly beats on HBM/DRAM and advanced logic inspection; guidance raised both quarters.

shifts.note: Consecutive quarters of above-consensus revenue on HBM and N2/N3 inspection demand.
shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 beat → Q3 FY26 beat → guidance raised both times; WFE cycle mid-upcycle

shifts.note: Gross margin ~58-60 % — sustained best-in-WFE; pricing power in process control maintained.
shifts.trend: GM 58-60 % across both quarters; services revenue mix improving margin floor

shifts.note: Management raised guidance at Q2 and Q3 — consecutive raise cadence.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q2 FY26 → guidance raised Q3 FY26; strong WFE cycle visibility

shifts.note: HBM inspection (DRAM stacking yield management) emerging as a fast-growing new segment.
shifts.trend: HBM inspection growing fastest; advanced packaging inspection adding to WFE-adjacent TAM

KLAC.verdict: KLAC is the best-positioned WFE company for the HBM cycle — process control is indispensable for yield management in multi-stack DRAM, and KLAC has no viable competitor at leading nodes. Consecutive beat-and-raise cadence supports the thesis.

LRCX.headline: Memory (DRAM/HBM) and logic etch/deposition driving consecutive beats; NAND recovery adding; guidance raised both quarters — WFE upcycle deepening.

shifts.note: Revenue beats and guidance raises at Q2 and Q3 — multi-quarter WFE upcycle.
shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 beat → Q3 FY26 beat → guidance raised both; HBM and logic both growing

shifts.note: DRAM/HBM leading; NAND recovering from trough; logic (GAA) adding incremental demand.
shifts.trend: HBM3/4 deposition tools primary driver; NAND etch recovering; GAA etch tools ramping

shifts.note: Consecutive quarterly guidance raises — WFE cycle has legs beyond initial HBM capex.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q2 → raised Q3; NAND recovery extends upcycle duration

shifts.note: Gross margin 47-48 % stable; services mix improving slowly.
shifts.trend: GM 47-48 % both quarters; services (spares, upgrades) growing as installed base expands

LRCX.verdict: Lam is the most exposed WFE name to the HBM build-out — every HBM stack requires multiple etch and deposition steps. NAND recovery adds duration. Consecutive beat-and-raise cadence confirms the cycle is mid-innings, not peaking.

PLTR.headline: US commercial AIP revenue growing 40-54 % YoY; Rule of 40 score 55-60+; consecutive guidance raises — Palantir is the clearest US AI software compounder.

shifts.note: US commercial growing 40-54 % YoY both quarters — AIP boot-camp model sustaining acceleration.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 US commercial +54 % YoY → Q1 2026 US commercial +40 % YoY (tougher comp, still accelerating vs. consensus)

shifts.note: Adjusted operating margin expanding to 45 %+; GAAP profitability now sustained.
shifts.trend: Adj. OM 40 % (Q4 2025) → 45 %+ (Q1 2026); GAAP net income positive both quarters

shifts.note: Management raised full-year US commercial and total revenue guidance at both earnings.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 2025 → raised Q1 2026; Rule of 40 score 55 → 60+

shifts.note: US commercial is the primary growth driver; US government resilient; international secondary.
shifts.trend: US commercial dominant; US gov mid-teens; international lagging — management focusing resources on US

PLTR.verdict: Palantir is the only enterprise AI software company with a proven enterprise-deployment methodology (AIP boot-camp) converting to 40-54 % US commercial revenue growth. Rule of 40 score 60+ is best-in-enterprise-software. The risk is whether government spending remains resilient.

NOW.headline: Now Assist AI driving ACV acceleration; subscription revenue growing 20-21 % YoY; Rule of 40 at 55; RPO growing ahead of revenue — best visibility in enterprise SaaS.

shifts.note: Subscription revenue growing 20-21 % YoY both quarters — Now Assist sustaining acceleration.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 subscription +21 % YoY → Q1 2026 subscription +22 % YoY — slight re-acceleration

shifts.note: FY2026 subscription revenue guidance raised at both earnings on Now Assist ACV momentum.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 2025 → raised Q1 2026; FY2026 guided 20 %+ growth

shifts.note: FCF margin stable >25 %; Rule of 40 score 55 — consistently best-in-class.
shifts.trend: FCF margin 25-28 % both quarters; Rule of 40 maintained at 55+

shifts.note: Federal/government segment strong both quarters — ITSM standardisation across agencies.
shifts.trend: Federal growing; enterprise commercial stable; Now Assist AI SKU becoming standard in new deals

NOW.verdict: ServiceNow is executing the most reliable enterprise AI monetisation in the dashboard — Now Assist is now standard in majority of new deals. Rule of 40 at 55+, guidance rising, RPO growing ahead of revenue. No thesis-break risk visible.

COHR.headline: 800G/1.6T datacom transceiver revenue growing 50 %+ YoY both quarters; AI-fabric demand backlog extending into 2027; SiC EV segment soft; gross margin recovery on track.

shifts.note: Datacom transceiver (AI networking) growing 50 %+ YoY both quarters — sustained acceleration.
shifts.trend: FQ2 FY26 datacom +50 %+ YoY → FQ3 FY26 datacom sustained; backlog extending into CY2027

shifts.note: Gross margin recovery progressing as integration costs roll off and AI-mix rises.
shifts.trend: GM recovering from integration-drag lows; AI datacom (higher margin) mixing up each quarter

shifts.note: Revenue guidance raised at FQ2 and maintained/raised at FQ3 on datacom strength.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised FQ2 → maintained/raised FQ3; datacom offsetting SiC and telecom softness

shifts.note: Datacom accelerating; SiC (EV) soft both quarters; telecom recovering slowly.
shifts.trend: Datacom dominant; SiC under pressure; telecom ROADM recovering; mix shifting toward AI datacom

COHR.verdict: Coherent's AI-driven thesis is executing — 800G/1.6T datacom growing 50 %+ YoY for two consecutive quarters with backlog extending into 2027. SiC softness is a headwind but not a thesis-breaker. Gross margin recovery is the key metric to watch: if it reaches 40 %+ by FY27, the investment case fully reprices.

LITE.headline: 800G/1.6T datacom transceiver demand strong both quarters; AI-fabric design wins at top-4 hyperscalers; telecom recovering slowly; margin improving on AI mix.

shifts.note: Datacom transceiver growing on AI-fabric demand; guidance raised FQ2 → FQ3.
shifts.trend: AI datacom revenue growing each quarter; hyperscaler design wins for next-gen switching fabrics confirmed

shifts.note: Gross margin improving as higher-ASP AI datacom products mix up vs. lower-margin telecom.
shifts.trend: GM improving sequentially as datacom (higher margin) grows and telecom (lower margin) is flat

shifts.note: FQ3 guidance raised at FQ2 earnings; FQ4 guidance raised at FQ3 on sustained AI demand.
shifts.trend: Sequential guidance raises correlating with AI transceiver demand visibility extending

shifts.note: Datacom (AI) accelerating; telecom recovering; consumer (VCSEL) stable — mix improving.
shifts.trend: Datacom AI dominant growth driver; telecom recovering but secondary; consumer stable

LITE.verdict: Lumentum's AI transceiver thesis is executing — design wins at top-4 hyperscalers for 800G/1.6T products, with backlog extending. Telecom softness is the headline risk but no longer a thesis-breaker given AI datacom scale. Margin expansion is the remaining catalyst for re-rating.

FN.headline: AI datacom transceiver manufacturing revenue growing strongly both quarters; no tariff exposure; capacity expanding; guidance raised at both earnings.

shifts.note: Revenue growing strongly on AI datacom manufacturing volumes; consecutive guidance raises.
shifts.trend: FQ2 FY26 beat → FQ3 FY26 beat → guidance raised both times; 800G/1.6T manufacturing the driver

shifts.note: Gross margin stable with operating leverage improving on revenue scale.
shifts.trend: GM stable; operating margin expanding as revenue grows faster than opex

shifts.note: Capacity expansion underway; Thailand facilities adding transceiver manufacturing lines.
shifts.trend: Capacity expansion both quarters to meet sustained AI transceiver demand

shifts.note: Thailand manufacturing not subject to US-China tariffs on AI optics (electronics exemptions).
shifts.trend: No material tariff risk disclosed; Thailand remains the key manufacturing location

FN.verdict: Fabrinet is the cleanest pure-play on AI transceiver volume growth — asset-light, capital-efficient, no tariff risk. Revenue growing as fast as COHR/LITE are designing product, with better execution certainty. The risk is customer concentration and margin pressure from transceiver OEM pricing.

GEV.headline: Gas turbine orders at record for two consecutive quarters; grid solutions backlog growing; power segment margins expanding; data-center power is the dominant demand driver.

shifts.note: Gas turbine orders at records Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 — data-center power driving a multi-year supercycle.
shifts.trend: Gas turbine orders: Q4 2025 record → Q1 2026 new record; backlog extending to 3-4 years on large frames

shifts.note: Power segment gross margin expanding on pricing power as lead times extend and backlog grows.
shifts.trend: Power margin expanding QoQ; FCF turning positive as backlog monetises

shifts.note: FY2026 power segment guidance raised at Q4 2025 and reaffirmed/raised at Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: Power guidance raised Q4 → raised Q1 2026; wind segment execution improving

shifts.note: Power (gas turbines) dominant; Grid solutions growing; Wind improving from 2024 execution issues.
shifts.trend: Power dominant; Grid growing (transformer lead times 2-3 years); Wind offshore improving

GEV.verdict: GE Vernova is the primary beneficiary of data-center power demand within the traditional industrial sector — gas turbines for AI power are creating a supercycle. Two consecutive record order quarters confirm durability. FCF turning positive is the next re-rating catalyst.

CAT.headline: Electric power (data-center generators) orders at multiyear highs both quarters; mining solid; construction mixed; pricing power intact.

shifts.note: Electric power division sustaining multiyear-high order rates Q4 2025 and Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: Electric power orders at multiyear highs both quarters; mining and infrastructure positive; residential soft

shifts.note: Adjusted operating margin >20 % both quarters — pricing power maintained.
shifts.trend: Operating margin >20 % both quarters; pricing power on large generator configurations maintained

shifts.note: FY2026 guidance maintained — data-center power offsetting construction softness.
shifts.trend: FY2026 guidance maintained; data-center power cited as key upside vector vs. macro uncertainty

shifts.note: Large generator lead times 18-24 months — supply constrained, not demand.
shifts.trend: Lead times 18-24 months on large (>2 MW) configurations; backlog at records

CAT.verdict: Caterpillar's data-center generator thesis is durable — two consecutive quarters of multiyear-high electric power orders with 18-24 month lead times. The story is not about CAT outperforming on AI; it is about diversified industrial exposure with a growing AI power tailwind that management is beginning to size explicitly.

CMI.headline: Power generation (data-center generators) backlog at records for two consecutive quarters; trucking softness offset by power demand; thesis intact.

shifts.note: Power generation growing on data-center generator demand; trucking engine softer; mix improving.
shifts.trend: Power generation backlog at records both quarters; trucking engine softening due to freight cycle

shifts.note: Power generation accelerating; trucking engine decelerating; distribution stable.
shifts.trend: Power gen dominant growth driver; truck engine soft; Accelera (zero-emissions) growing from small base

shifts.note: Management maintained FY2026 guidance — power generation offsetting trucking weakness.
shifts.trend: FY2026 guidance maintained both quarters; data-center power cited as multi-year visibility driver

shifts.note: Large stationary generator lead times 12-18 months — constrained supply on high demand.
shifts.trend: Generator lead times 12-18 months both quarters; backlog at records

CMI.verdict: Cummins power-generation thesis is intact — data-center backup generator demand is creating a multi-year revenue floor that offsets trucking cyclicality. Lead times 12-18 months provide revenue visibility. Accelera (fuel cell) is a long-term option with limited near-term impact.

NVT.headline: Data-center enclosures and liquid-cooling growing 30 %+ YoY both quarters; FY2026 guidance raised; direct liquid cooling (DLC) orders fastest-growing product.

shifts.note: Data-center segment growing 30 %+ YoY both quarters; DLC orders growing even faster.
shifts.trend: Data-center revenue +30 %+ YoY Q4 2025 → Q1 2026; DLC orders growing faster than air-cooling

shifts.note: Gross margin expanding as data-center (higher-margin) mixes up vs. industrial.
shifts.trend: GM expanding QoQ as data-center mix rises; industrial margins stable

shifts.note: FY2026 guidance raised at Q4 2025; maintained/raised at Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 → raised/maintained Q1 2026; data-center segment guided to accelerate

shifts.note: Backlog elevated; lead times extending on liquid-cooling products — demand-constrained, not supply.
shifts.trend: Backlog at records both quarters; capacity expansion underway for DLC products

NVT.verdict: nVent is a high-conviction data-center thermal and power management play — DLC is the fastest-growing product and the AI GPU density trend guarantees continued demand. Two consecutive quarters of 30 %+ growth with guidance raises validates the thesis.

HUBB.headline: Data-center and grid-modernisation demand driving best order environment in Hubbell history; transformer backlog 18-24 months; pricing power intact both quarters.

shifts.note: Best order environment in company history both quarters on data-center and grid demand.
shifts.trend: Order rates at records both quarters; both utility solutions and electrical products growing

shifts.note: Gross margin expanding on pricing power and favourable volume leverage.
shifts.trend: GM expanding QoQ; pricing power maintained in constrained transformer supply environment

shifts.note: FY2026 revenue and EPS guidance raised at both earnings.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 2025 → raised Q1 2026; data-center and grid cited as multi-year tailwinds

shifts.note: Transformer lead times 18-24 months — supply tightness benefits pricing power.
shifts.trend: Transformer lead times 18-24 months both quarters; switchgear lead times also extending

HUBB.verdict: Hubbell is executing the data-center and grid-modernisation thesis with consecutive beat-and-raise cadence. Transformer supply tightness (18-24 month leads) is compounding pricing power. The risk is macro-driven infrastructure spending slowdown, but backlog visibility extends into 2027.

PWR.headline: Backlog at record $35 B+ for two consecutive quarters; data-center grid interconnection is fastest-growing project type; guidance raised both quarters.

shifts.note: Revenue growing double-digits both quarters; backlog growing faster than revenue.
shifts.trend: Electric power revenue +double-digits YoY both quarters; backlog $35 B+ both quarters

shifts.note: Gross margin improving on favourable project mix — high-voltage, complex work at premium.
shifts.trend: GM improving as high-complexity (data-center interconnect, transmission) grows vs. lower-margin distribution

shifts.note: FY2026 revenue and EPS guidance raised at Q4 2025 and Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 → raised Q1 2026; backlog $35 B+ providing exceptional visibility

shifts.note: Skilled lineman labour is the primary constraint on project execution pace.
shifts.trend: Labour tightness noted both quarters; workforce expansion underway but multi-year to build

PWR.verdict: Quanta Services has the best revenue visibility in the dashboard — $35 B+ backlog at 1× annual revenue, growing. Data-center grid interconnection is the fastest-growing project type and is fundamentally different from historical transmission work in scale and complexity. Beat-and-raise cadence for two consecutive quarters confirms execution.

GNRC.headline: C&I (data-center) generator backlog at records for two consecutive quarters; residential recovering; guidance raised for C&I both quarters.

shifts.note: C&I (data-center generator) revenue growing double-digits both quarters; residential recovering.
shifts.trend: C&I +double-digits YoY both quarters; residential recovering from 2024 digestion; IQ8 storage growing

shifts.note: Gross margin recovering as C&I mix rises and residential pricing normalises.
shifts.trend: GM recovering QoQ as C&I (higher margin) grows faster; residential margin normalising

shifts.note: C&I guidance raised at Q4 2025 and Q1 2026; residential maintained.
shifts.trend: C&I guidance raised both quarters; residential maintained; total guidance raised

shifts.note: Large C&I generator lead times 15-18 months — supply tight on high demand.
shifts.trend: Lead times 15-18 months on large configurations both quarters; C&I backlog at records

GNRC.verdict: Generac's data-center C&I generator thesis is executing well — two consecutive quarters of record backlog with 15-18 month lead times and raised guidance. Residential recovery adds duration. The risk is if data-center customers transition away from diesel backup (toward battery or fuel cell) faster than expected.

CRM.headline: Agentforce launching; subscription revenue growing 8-9 % YoY; operating margins expanding; Data Cloud growing — AI inflection point approaching but not yet in numbers.

shifts.note: Subscription revenue growing 8-9 % YoY — consistent; Agentforce not yet material to numbers.
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 +8 % YoY → Q4 FY26 +9 % YoY; Agentforce pipeline growing but revenue nascent

shifts.note: Data Cloud CDP growing double-digits; Agentforce early-adopter deals signed.
shifts.trend: Data Cloud growing; Agentforce deals signed but contribution <1 % of revenue in Q4 FY26

shifts.note: Operating margin expanding sequentially — cost discipline while investing in AI.
shifts.trend: Adj. OM expanding each quarter; SG&A efficiency improving as revenue scales

shifts.note: FY27 guidance $40.5-40.9 B (+8-9 % YoY) — conservative; Agentforce upside not baked in.
shifts.trend: FY27 guidance issued at Q4 FY26; Agentforce upside is the key beat catalyst

CRM.verdict: CRM is at an Agentforce inflection point — the product has launched, customer pipeline is growing, but revenue contribution is not yet visible in the P&L. The FY27 guidance of 8-9 % growth is conservative if Agentforce converts at the rate management describes. Data Cloud is the enabling layer that could accelerate adoption.

SNPS.headline: EDA growing 13-14 % YoY on AI-chip design surge; Ansys acquisition pending regulatory approval; Palladium/Protium emulation platforms at capacity.

shifts.note: EDA revenue growing 13-14 % YoY both periods — AI-chip design volume driving consistent growth.
shifts.trend: FY25 full-year EDA revenue +13-14 % YoY; Q1 FY26 consistent; AI design activity sustained

shifts.note: Ansys acquisition ($35 B) pending — creates EDA + simulation combined platform.
shifts.trend: Ansys regulatory review ongoing; EU/UK approval expected H1 FY26; combined entity changes TAM

shifts.note: Gross margin >80 %; operating margin >30 % — consistent software-model profitability.
shifts.trend: GM >80 % both periods; OM >30 % — stable subscription EDA model economics

shifts.note: FY26 standalone guidance double-digits; Ansys closing changes combined entity outlook.
shifts.trend: Standalone FY26 guided double-digit growth; Ansys adds $2 B+ revenue upon closing

SNPS.verdict: Synopsys is the duopolist EDA platform for AI chip design — every AI silicon project requires EDA tools. Growing 13-14 % YoY with consistent margins and a clear path to accelerating upon Ansys close. The thesis is durable and low-risk relative to semiconductor equipment or chip names.

CDNS.headline: EDA growing mid-to-high teens YoY both quarters; Palladium/Protium emulation platforms fully booked with multi-quarter lead times; guidance raised both quarters.

shifts.note: EDA revenue growing mid-to-high teens YoY both quarters — AI-chip design sustaining demand.
shifts.trend: FY25 +14 % YoY → Q1 2026 +14-15 % YoY; AI tapeout volume and complexity both rising

shifts.note: Palladium emulation platforms fully booked; multi-quarter lead times — constrained supply.
shifts.trend: Palladium/Protium lead times extended both quarters; emulation capacity fully allocated to top AI chip designs

shifts.note: Full-year 2026 guidance raised at Q4 FY25 and reaffirmed/raised at Q1 2026.
shifts.trend: Guidance raised Q4 FY25 → maintained/raised Q1 2026; backlog visibility strong

shifts.note: Gross margin >85 %; operating margin >30 % — consistent high-margin software economics.
shifts.trend: GM >85 % both quarters; OM improving on operating leverage

CDNS.verdict: Cadence is the cleaner EDA play vs. Synopsys (no Ansys execution risk) and is executing the AI-chip design thesis with consecutive guided beats. Palladium emulation supply tightness is a revenue constraint and an investment opportunity signal — when emulation is sold out, chip design activity is at peak intensity.

HPE.headline: AI server (Cray, ProLiant Gen12) growing on hyperscaler and research demand; GreenLake ARR growing; Juniper integration adding network synergies.

shifts.note: AI server revenue growing both quarters; total company growth modest given mix.
shifts.trend: AI server (Cray) growing strongly; total company revenue growing low-to-mid single digits on legacy segment drag

shifts.note: AI server dominant growth driver; Intelligent Edge recovering; Storage growing on AI workload demand.
shifts.trend: AI server accelerating; Intelligent Edge recovering; Storage growing; legacy servers declining

shifts.note: Gross margin improving as AI servers (higher ASP) and GreenLake software attach rates rise.
shifts.trend: GM improving QoQ as AI server and software mix rises; Juniper integration accretive to margin

shifts.note: FY2026 guidance maintained at both earnings; AI server backlog providing near-term visibility.
shifts.trend: FY2026 guidance maintained; AI server backlog cited as primary visibility driver

HPE.verdict: HPE is a leveraged play on AI infrastructure buildout — not an AI-native company but a compute infrastructure manufacturer with growing AI server content. Juniper adds a meaningful networking overlay. The risk is margin dilution if AI server pricing becomes competitive.

SNOW.headline: Only one new filing; insufficient for cross-quarter trend (single 8-K, Q3 FY26). Cortex AI driving product revenue +28 % YoY; NRR 127 %.

shifts.note: Product revenue +28 % YoY — Cortex AI and Snowpark workloads driving re-acceleration.
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 product revenue +28 % YoY; NRR 127 %; Cortex AI consumption growing

SNOW.verdict: Single filing — insufficient for cross-quarter trend analysis. Cortex AI is the key watch item: if LLM inference consumption on Snowflake continues to grow, it could re-rate the product revenue growth trajectory materially.

CLS.headline: Single new 8-K — Celestica AI server and networking hardware revenue growing rapidly; hyperscaler manufacturing orders at records; guidance raised.

shifts.note: CCS (AI server rack assembly) revenue growing strongly; guidance raised for FY2026.
shifts.trend: AI server and networking hardware manufacturing revenue growing; hyperscaler customer orders at records

shifts.note: Gross margin expanding as AI hardware (higher-value) mixes up vs. commodity server assembly.
shifts.trend: GM expanding as AI hardware revenue grows faster than commodity server assembly

CLS.verdict: Celestica is a high-leverage play on hyperscaler AI server manufacturing outsourcing. AI hardware is now the dominant revenue driver. Guidance raised on AI order momentum.

MOD.headline: Data-center liquid-cooling revenue growing 40 %+ YoY for two consecutive quarters; CDU orders at records; guidance raised at both earnings.

shifts.note: Data-center cooling growing 40 %+ YoY both quarters — AI GPU density driving CDU demand.
shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 data-center +40 %+ YoY → Q3 FY26 sustained; CDU orders at records both quarters

shifts.note: GM expanding as data-center cooling (higher-margin) mixes up vs. HVAC.
shifts.trend: GM expanding QoQ as data-center segment grows faster than HVAC

shifts.note: Data-center revenue guidance raised at Q2 and Q3.
shifts.trend: Data-center guidance raised Q2 → raised Q3; HVAC maintained; total guidance raised

MOD.verdict: Modine is a pure-play liquid-cooling beneficiary with two consecutive quarters of 40 %+ data-center growth. CDU orders at records with capacity expansion underway. The HVAC segment provides stability while data-center drives growth.

INTC.headline: 18A process (2nm-class) yield improving; Panther Lake on track for H2 2026; IFS external revenue growing; Gaudi AI accelerator gaining traction — credibility events approaching.

shifts.note: Revenue stabilising after multi-year share loss; 18A yield progress is the key catalyst.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 revenue stabilising; Q1 2026 recovery; 18A Panther Lake risk production H1 2026

shifts.note: Gross margin recovering from 2024 lows as foundry losses narrow and product mix improves.
shifts.trend: GM recovering both quarters; IFS losses still material but narrowing

shifts.note: IFS (foundry) external revenue growing; Gaudi AI traction at Meta/Amazon; Altera strategic review.
shifts.trend: IFS external customers growing; Gaudi 3 gaining enterprise traction; Altera sale/IPO possible

shifts.note: Management maintained Q1 2026 guidance; 18A yield is the key near-term catalyst.
shifts.trend: Guidance maintained both quarters; 18A yield progress cited as primary positive signal

INTC.verdict: Intel is still in recovery mode — 18A process is the credibility event that determines whether the thesis fully reprices. Two quarters of stabilising revenue and improving 18A yield language are positive signals. Gaudi AI adoption and IFS external revenue are the incremental growth levers. High execution risk remains.

CRWV.headline: First public earnings post-IPO; GPU cloud revenue growing rapidly on Microsoft/OpenAI anchor contracts; NVDA backing provides GPU allocation advantage; capex-intensive model.

shifts.note: Revenue growing rapidly both quarters on hyperscaler AI workload demand.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 pre-IPO revenue growing → Q1 2026 first public quarter; hyperscaler contracts driving

shifts.note: Microsoft and OpenAI represent majority of revenue — two-customer concentration risk.
shifts.trend: Top-2 customers dominant both quarters; customer diversification is the multi-year execution goal

shifts.note: GPU cluster capex-intensive model; lease and equipment obligations primary balance sheet item.
shifts.trend: Capex growing both quarters to expand GPU cluster capacity; IPO proceeds fund expansion

shifts.note: Gross margin improving as fixed-cost absorption improves on revenue scale.
shifts.trend: GM improving QoQ as GPU cluster utilisation rises and fixed costs amortise over more revenue

CRWV.verdict: CoreWeave is a high-risk, high-reward AI infrastructure bet — the only pure-play GPU cloud company with hyperscaler-scale contracts. Customer concentration (Microsoft + OpenAI) and capex intensity are the primary risks. NVDA equity backing and GPU allocation priority are the key competitive moats.

LEU.headline: HALEU production advancing; DOE contract Phase 2 negotiations ongoing; SMR developer engagement growing — only US domestic HALEU enricher.

shifts.note: HALEU production milestones advancing both quarters; SMR fuel supply pipeline growing.
shifts.trend: HALEU production continuing; DOE Phase 2 contract negotiations advancing; SMR engagement growing

shifts.note: Russia TENEX wind-down creating domestic supply urgency — LEU is the only US option.
shifts.trend: Russia supply wind-down progressing; US domestic enrichment urgency growing both quarters

shifts.note: SMR developer diversification improving — TerraPower, X-energy, OKLO in discussions.
shifts.trend: Multiple SMR developers engaging for fuel supply; not solely dependent on single DOE contract

LEU.verdict: LEU is a speculative but uniquely positioned nuclear fuel play — the only US domestic HALEU enricher in a post-Russia supply world. The thesis requires SMR commercialisation (2028-2030) to materialise. DOE Phase 2 contract is the near-term catalyst; OKLO/TerraPower deployment timelines are the long-term drivers.

MXL.headline: PAM4 DSP and ethernet PHY recovering from 2024 digestion; AI networking silicon design wins converting; revenue growing sequentially each quarter of 2026.

shifts.note: Revenue recovering from 2024 trough; management guiding sequential growth each quarter.
shifts.trend: Q4 2025 recovering → Q1 2026 sequential growth; Spica Gen2 and Sleipnir design wins converting

shifts.note: Gross margin recovering as AI networking silicon (higher ASP) mixes up.
shifts.trend: GM recovering from trough as revenue mix shifts toward higher-ASP AI networking silicon

shifts.note: Management guiding sequential growth each quarter of 2026 — recovery confidence growing.
shifts.trend: Sequential growth guidance maintained both quarters; design win conversion providing visibility

MXL.verdict: MaxLinear is in early-cycle recovery — the inventory digestion is behind it and AI networking silicon design wins are converting. The next 3-4 quarters of sequential revenue growth will determine whether MXL re-rates as an AI networking compounder or remains a cyclical recovery story.
